title: Dogfood the full cross-branch workflow and prove the shared-ref authority plane materially improves daily development UX
summary: Validate the whole end-state against the pain points that motivated this work: two branches seeing the same claims and status, strict require publishing without false completion, target-sensitive follow-up work gating on integrated_to_target when needed, and PRISM surfaces staying legible during concurrent live development.

acceptance.label: Cross-branch dogfooding confirms that claims, publish state, and integration state converge through the shared ref rather than branch-local accidents.
The resulting workflow materially improves developer UX for concurrent branches, shared task visibility, and target-sensitive coordination.
